prompt: How much does protein feeding increase muscle protein synthesis compared with resistance training itself? Are we over-focusing on protein source and timing details?
expected_output: They said lifting is the dominant driver of muscle protein synthesis, while protein strategy is more of a permissive support factor. Eric used examples from androgen-deprivation and immobilization research to show how large the training effect can be relative to nutrition tweaks. During layoffs, they noted that even very reduced training volume (roughly 10-15% of normal, i.e., an 85-90% cut) can still help preserve muscle and strength. By contrast, protein-only interventions during immobilization are usually underwhelming, with mixed small benefits in some higher-dose amino acid/leucine studies. Their practical hierarchy was to keep some safe muscle loading whenever possible, then optimize protein amount/distribution/timing on top of that.
assertions: Answer states lifting is the dominant driver of muscle protein synthesis, protein is permissive support
Answer mentions even very reduced training volume (10-15% of normal) can help preserve muscle
Answer notes protein-only interventions during immobilization are usually underwhelming
Answer recommends keeping some safe muscle loading whenever possible, then optimizing protein on top
